LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED ITS BURDEN TO SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF FAILED TO FILE THE REQUISITE GOVERNMENT CLAIM UNDER GOV. CODE 905, 905.1, 905.2, AND 911.2(A) BEFORE FILING THIS SUIT. PLAINTIFF CONCEDES IN HIS LETTER TO THE COURT THAT HE DID NOT FILE SUCH CLAIM. THE COURT DENIES PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE BECAUSE THERE IS NOTHING THAT WOULD CHANGE THE FACTS OF THIS CASE WHICH REQUIRE THAT THE MOTION BE GRANTED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: marcell patrick neri, esq., 415.288.9800, for defendant the regents of the university of california; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted. No opposition filed. The demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Pltf'S Complaint For Damages;
Off Calendar Jun-18-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff'S Complaint For Damages as to Does 1-30 is Granted. Does 1-30 are dismissed from this action pursuant to CCP section 583.250. No opposition was filed to this motion. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Off Calendar Dec-24-2012 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-07-2012 Set For Case Management Conference On Jan-09-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-07-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - GEORGE WASHINGTON CLARK III, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. DAVID W. AMMONS, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3954 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted its burden to show that Plaintiff failed to file the requisite government claim under Gov. Code 905, 905.1, 905.2, and 911.2(a) before filing this suit. Plaintiff concedes in his letter to the Court that he did not file such claim. The Court denies Plaintiff's request for a continuance because there is nothing that would change the facts of this case which require that the motion be granted. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(G). Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976
